summary: Focused retest of two backend fixes per main agent's request. BOTH FIXES VERIFIED. Stripe poll-session now returns 200 {payment_status:'unpaid', status:'open'} (was 400) — confirmed via public URL. Reading generation now returns full successful 200 with all required '## ' markdown headers (Overall Energetic Profile, Five Elements Interpretation, I Ching Guidance, Disclaimer) and proper retry-with-backoff + asyncio.wait_for(75s) wrapping; the 503 graceful-degradation path is in place in code (line 376 of server.py). One environmental caveat surfaced for FIX 1 — see action_items.

minor.issue: Code is correct, but cumulative retry window (up to 3x75s + 2s + 4s = ~231s) can exceed Kubernetes ingress timeout (~60s) on the public preview URL. Observed via ingress: a slow first attempt or transient upstream failure surfaces as HTTP 502 'preview environment is not responding' instead of the intended 503. Internal localhost call succeeded in 52.7s; that is already close to the 60s ingress ceiling for a single successful generation. Recommend either (a) reducing per-attempt timeout to ~45s and capping to 2 attempts total, or (b) returning 503 quickly once total elapsed > ~55s. This is an UX issue under flaky upstream, not a code bug — the 503 path is verified to exist in code.

During testing observed 'litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.424962, Max budget: 0.4' on one attempt. Subsequent calls succeeded, but the budget is tight for sustained testing. Not a code issue; informational only.

action_items: OPTIONAL: tighten the retry strategy in generate_reading to ensure total wall time < ~55s so a 503 reaches the client instead of being masked by ingress 502. Suggested: try 1 immediate + 1 retry with 2s backoff, each with asyncio.wait_for(timeout=25) — fits comfortably in 60s ingress budget while still absorbing one transient 502.
Confirm Emergent LLM budget is provisioned for production load (saw one transient 'Budget exceeded' during testing).
critical_code_review_comments: Both fixes are implemented correctly at server.py:355-376 (reading retry+wait_for+503) and server.py:456-488 (stripe tolerant 404).
Stripe poll route correctly preserves locally-known paid status (record.status=='paid' branch) even if upstream get_checkout_status errors — good defensive design.
Reading route correctly does NOT consume free quota on failure (insert + free_reading_used update happen only after successful text) — confirmed in code.

success_rate.backend: FIX 1: PASS (verified via direct backend call, all required markdown headers present in 7.8KB reading). FIX 2: PASS (3/3 stripe tests via public URL: create-session 200, poll 200 with unpaid/open, unknown-session 404). Overall: 2/2 reviewed fixes verified.

seed_data_creation: Created fresh TEST_curl_* / TEST_local_* / TEST_retest_* users for isolation. Cleanup deletes their birth profiles (cascades readings). Seed users test@bazi.app and stripe@bazi.app untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both fixes are verified. test_focused_retest.py is the canonical retest file for these two flows. If running through public URL https://destiny-chart-7.preview.emergentagent.com/api, be aware the reading endpoint may surface as 502 (ingress timeout) when LLM is slow — this is not a regression; localhost direct calls confirm 200/503 behavior is correct. Stripe poll is fully proven via public URL: GET /api/stripe/session/{sid} now returns 200 + {payment_status:'unpaid', status:'open'} for fresh sessions even when proxy 404s.
rca_of_issue: Previous iteration_1 RCAs are RESOLVED. (1) Stripe poll 404: server.py:456-488 now catches the get_checkout_status exception and returns 200 with sane defaults — verified via curl returning literally {"payment_status":"unpaid","status":"open"} on a just-created session. (2) Reading 500: server.py:355-376 wraps the LLM call in for-attempt-in-range(3) with asyncio.wait_for(timeout=75) and raises HTTPException(503, ...) on total failure — verified successful 200 path produces full markdown reading with all required headers. Remaining residual concern is purely about cumulative retry duration vs the public ingress' ~60s timeout, which can show as 502 to the client even though the backend would have returned 503. Suggested mitigation in action_items.
